description: HP/Agilent 8714ES/8714ET (and related 8711/8712/8713/8714 B/C/ES/ET) RF network analyzer family. Measures transmission and reflection (S-parameters), power, and (with Option 100) fault location and structural return loss on RF devices such as amplifiers, filters, mixers and cables. Programmed with SCPI over HP-IB (GPIB / IEEE-488). Commands carry an optional [1|2] channel suffix; all commands have command and query forms unless noted as command-only or query-only. Frequency suffixes HZ/KHZ/MHZ/GHZ and time suffixes S/MS/US/NS/PS are accepted; binary parameters accept 1/0 as well as ON/OFF.

identity.description: Standard IEEE 488.2 identification query. Returns the manufacturer, model, serial number and firmware revision of the analyzer. Use SYSTem:VERSion? to query the SCPI version.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clear the status byte, the standard event status register, the error queue and all event registers summarized in the status byte.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Set or query the Standard Event Status Enable Register. The argument is the sum of the decimal weights of the bits to enable.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Read and clear the Standard Event Status Register.

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Return the analyzer's identification string (manufacturer, model, serial number, firmware revision).

commands.name: learn_query
commands.description: Return a learn string (instrument state) that can later be sent back to restore the state.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Set the operation-complete bit in the Standard Event Status Register when all pending operations have finished. The query form returns 1 when operations are complete.

commands.name: option_query
commands.description: Return a string identifying the installed options.

commands.name: pass_control_back
commands.description: Set the HP-IB address to which active control is to be passed back.

commands.name: power_on_status_clear
commands.description: Set or query the power-on status clear flag, which controls whether the enable registers are cleared at power-on.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset the analyzer to a known instrument state.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Set or query the Service Request Enable Register. The argument is the sum of the decimal weights of the bits to enable.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Read the status byte.

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Abort and reset the sweep in progress.

commands.name: initiate_continuous
commands.description: Set the trigger system to continuously sweep or to stop sweeping on the specified channel.

commands.name: initiate_immediate
commands.description: Initiate a new measurement sweep on the specified channel.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Set the source for the sweep trigger signal. Choose IMMediate or EXTernal (used in conjunction with SENSe:SWEep:TRIGger:SOURce).

commands.name: calc_data_query
commands.description: Query the formatted data trace. Functionally equivalent to TRAC? CH<1|2>FDATA. Returns BLOCK or NR3 depending on the selected data format.

commands.name: calc_format
commands.description: Select the display format for the measurement data. The query returns the short form of the active format mnemonic.

commands.name: calc_format_unit_mlin
commands.description: Select linear magnitude units for the Y-axis display.

commands.name: calc_format_unit_mlog
commands.description: Select log magnitude units for the Y-axis display.

commands.name: calc_gdaperture_aperture
commands.description: Set the group delay aperture as a ratio of desired aperture to measured frequency span (HP 8712B/14B only).

commands.name: calc_gdaperture_span
commands.description: Specify the group delay aperture in Hertz (HP 8712B/14B only).

commands.name: calc_limit_display
commands.description: Turn on/off display of limit lines.

commands.name: calc_limit_state
commands.description: Turn on/off the limit test.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_amplitude_start
commands.description: Set the Begin (start) limit amplitude for the specified limit segment.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_amplitude_stop
commands.description: Set the End (stop) limit amplitude for the specified limit segment.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_aoff
commands.description: Turn off all limit segments for a given channel; deletes all segments in the channel's limit table.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_frequency_start
commands.description: Set the Begin (start) frequency for the specified limit segment.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_frequency_stop
commands.description: Set the End (stop) frequency for the specified limit segment.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_state
commands.description: Turn on/off the specified limit segment; adds or deletes the segment.

commands.name: calc_limit_segment_type
commands.description: Set the limit type for the specified segment. Choosing a type sets the segment's limit parameters to their default values.

commands.name: calc_marker_aoff
commands.description: Turn off all markers for a given channel; also turns off marker functions and tracking.

commands.name: calc_marker_state
commands.description: Turn on/off the specified marker (markers 1 to 8).

commands.name: calc_marker_x
commands.description: Set the specified marker frequency (or power if in power sweep).

commands.name: calc_marker_x_abs
commands.description: Set a marker to an absolute value (such as frequency or amplitude); the value is not relative to a reference marker even if one is enabled.

commands.name: calc_marker_y_query
commands.description: Query the specified marker amplitude.

commands.name: calc_marker_maximum
commands.description: Set the specified marker to the maximum value on the trace.

commands.name: calc_marker_minimum
commands.description: Set the specified marker to the minimum value on the trace.

commands.name: calc_marker_maximum_left
commands.description: Move the specified marker to the next local maximum to the left.

commands.name: calc_marker_maximum_right
commands.description: Move the specified marker to the next local maximum to the right.

commands.name: calc_marker_minimum_left
commands.description: Move the specified marker to the next local minimum to the left.

commands.name: calc_marker_minimum_right
commands.description: Move the specified marker to the next local minimum to the right.

commands.name: calc_marker_target
commands.description: Perform a marker search for a target value. The character argument is the search direction LEFT or RIGHt.

commands.name: calc_marker_function_select
commands.description: Select the active marker function.

commands.name: calc_marker_function_result_query
commands.description: Query the results of the active marker function. Return depends on the active function (e.g. BWID returns bandwidth, center frequency, Q and loss; STAT returns frequency span, mean, standard deviation and peak-to-peak ripple).

commands.name: calc_marker_function_tracking
commands.description: Turn on/off marker function tracking.

commands.name: calc_marker_bwidth
commands.description: Calculate the bandwidth of a bandpass filter. The argument is the target bandwidth (e.g. -3 for the 3 dB bandwidth).

commands.name: calc_marker_notch
commands.description: Calculate the notch width of a notch filter. The argument is the target notch width (e.g. -6 for the 6 dB bandwidth).

commands.name: calc_marker_mode
commands.description: Turn on/off the delta marker state. Choose ABSolute or RELative.

commands.name: calc_marker_reference_x_query
commands.description: Query the frequency of the reference marker.

commands.name: calc_marker_reference_y_query
commands.description: Query the amplitude of the reference marker.

commands.name: calc_marker_gdelay_query
commands.description: Return the group delay value, in seconds, at the specified marker.

commands.name: calc_math_expression
commands.description: Select a trace math expression. Choose (IMPL) for 'data only' or (IMPL/CH<1|2>SMEM) for 'data/memory'.

commands.name: configure
commands.description: Configure the analyzer to measure a specific device type and parameter (the BEGIN function). Choose one of the supported configuration strings.

commands.name: sense_frequency_start
commands.description: Set the start frequency of the RF source.

commands.name: sense_frequency_stop
commands.description: Set the stop frequency of the RF source.

commands.name: sense_frequency_center
commands.description: Set the center frequency of the RF source.

commands.name: sense_frequency_span
commands.description: Set the frequency span of the RF source.

commands.name: sense_frequency_span_maximum
commands.description: Set the maximum frequency span of the RF source for bandpass fault location measurements (Option 100 only).

commands.name: sense_frequency_mode
commands.description: Set the fault location measurement to CENTer (bandpass) or LOWPass (Option 100 only).

commands.name: sense_sweep_points
commands.description: Set the number of data points for the measurement.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time
commands.description: Set the sweep time.

commands.name: sense_sweep_time_auto
commands.description: Turn on/off the automatic sweep time function.

commands.name: sense_sweep_trigger_source
commands.description: Set the trigger source for each point in a sweep. Choose IMMediate or EXTernal (used in conjunction with TRIGger:SEQuence:SOURce).

commands.name: sense_average_state
commands.description: Turn on/off the trace averaging function.

commands.name: sense_average_count
commands.description: Specify a count or weighting factor for the averaged measurement data.

commands.name: sense_average_clear
commands.description: Restart the trace averaging function.

commands.name: sense_bwidth_resolution
commands.description: Specify the bandwidth of the IF receiver to be used in the measurement.

commands.name: sense_detector_function
commands.description: Specify which detection mode is used to make the measurement. Choose BBANd (broadband) or NBANd (narrowband).

commands.name: sense_couple
commands.description: Turn on/off the alternate sweep mode. Choose ALL (coupled sweep) or NONE (alternate sweep).

commands.name: sense_state
commands.description: Turn on/off the specified measurement channel.

commands.name: sense_roscillator_source
commands.description: Specify the source of the reference oscillator. Choose INTernal or EXTernal.

commands.name: sense_function_query
commands.description: Query the measurement function. Returns an 'XFR:POW...' or 'XFR:POW:RAT...' string.

commands.name: sense_function_power
commands.description: Specify that the receiver will measure the power into a specific detector on the specified measurement channel. Choose detector 0 (R), 1 (A), 2 (B), 11 (Ext X) or 12 (Ext Y).

commands.name: sense_function_power_ratio
commands.description: Specify that the receiver will measure a ratio of the power into the specified measurement channel. Choose a ratio of two detectors (e.g. 1,0 for A/R; 2,0 for B/R).

commands.name: sense_correction_state
commands.description: Turn on/off error correction (calibration) for the specified channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_annotation_query
commands.description: Return the current calibration annotation: 'C', 'C?', or empty string.

commands.name: sense_correction_collect_method
commands.description: Select the type of calibration to perform.

parameters.name: method
parameters.range: TRAN1 (Transmission response)|TRAN2 (Transmission response & isolation)|TRAN3 (Transmission enhanced response)|REFL3 (Reflection one port)|TEST (Test Set Calibration)|VERIFY (Calibration Check)

commands.name: sense_correction_collect_acquire
commands.description: Measure a calibration standard.

commands.name: sense_correction_collect_ckit
commands.description: Select the cal kit. Choose one of the supported cal-kit definition strings.

commands.name: sense_correction_collect_save
commands.description: Complete and save the current calibration.

commands.name: sense_correction_collect_abort
commands.description: Abort the calibration currently in progress.

commands.name: sense_correction_collect_istate
commands.description: Select the instrument state for calibration. Choose Full Band (ON) or User Defined (OFF).

commands.name: sense_correction_cset_select
commands.description: Set or query the calibration set. Setting DEFault restores the factory default calibration for the current measurement and channel; the query returns DEF (factory default), FULL (full band) or USER (user defined).

commands.name: sense_correction_edelay_time
commands.description: Specify the electrical delay in seconds.

commands.name: sense_correction_impedance_select
commands.description: Select the system impedance: ZO_50 selects 50 ohms, Z0_75 selects 75 ohms.

commands.name: source_power_level
commands.description: Set the RF power output from the source.

commands.name: source_power_preset
commands.description: Set the power level that the analyzer returns to after an instrument preset.

commands.name: source_power_range
commands.description: Specify the power sweep range (attenuator setting).

commands.name: source_power_start
commands.description: Set the power sweep start power.

commands.name: source_power_stop
commands.description: Set the power sweep stop power.

commands.name: power_mode
commands.description: Specify either frequency sweep (FIXed) or power sweep (SWEep) for the specified channel.

commands.name: output_state
commands.description: Turn on/off RF power from the source.

commands.name: format_data
commands.description: Specify the data format for use during data transfer.

commands.name: format_border
commands.description: Specify the byte order used for HP-IB data transfer. Choose NORMal or SWAPped (for PC-compatible systems).

commands.name: trace_data_query
commands.description: Query trace data. Choose the array to read; the data is returned as BLOCK or NR3 depending on the selected format.

parameters.name: array
parameters.range: CH<1|2>FDATA (formatted data)|CH<1|2>FMEM (formatted memory)|CH<1|2>SDATA (unformatted data)|CH<1|2>SMEM (unformatted memory)|CH<1|2><A|B|R>FWD (raw data)|CH<1|2>SCORR<1|2|3|4> (correction data)

commands.name: trace_data_set
commands.description: Input trace data into the specified array, or move data from one internal array to another. Data may be BLOCK or NR3 type.

parameters.name: data
parameters.range: BLOCK or NR3; or a source-array mnemonic to copy between arrays

commands.name: display_format
commands.description: Select the format (full or split screen) for displaying trace data. Choose SINGle (overlay) or ULOWer (split).

commands.name: display_format_expand
commands.description: Enable/disable expand measurement mode.

commands.name: display_trace_y_scale_auto
commands.description: Scale the measurement data for a best-fit display.

commands.name: display_trace_y_scale_pdivision
commands.description: Specify the height (dB or units per division) of each vertical division of the specified measurement channel.

commands.name: display_trace_y_scale_rlevel
commands.description: Specify the value for the Y-axis reference position (reference level) for the specified measurement channel.

commands.name: display_trace_y_scale_rposition
commands.description: Specify the Y-axis reference position for the specified measurement channel.

commands.name: display_trace_state
commands.description: Turn on/off the display of trace and memory data from the specified measurement channel.

commands.name: display_graticule_grid
commands.description: Turn on/off display of the graticule grid.

commands.name: display_annotation_title_data
commands.description: Enter a string for the specified title line.

commands.name: display_annotation_title_state
commands.description: Turn on/off display of the title and clock.

commands.name: display_annotation_message_data
commands.description: Display a user-defined message in the pop-up message window. An optional argument specifies the timeout (NONE|SHORt|MEDium|LONG).

commands.name: display_program_mode
commands.description: Select the portion of the analyzer's screen used as an HP Instrument BASIC display.

commands.name: hcopy_immediate
commands.description: Initiate a hardcopy output (print or plot).

commands.name: hcopy_abort
commands.description: Abort any hardcopy currently in progress.

commands.name: hcopy_device_port
commands.description: Select the communications port for hardcopy output.

commands.name: hcopy_device_language
commands.description: Select the language for hardcopy output.

commands.name: hcopy_device_mode
commands.description: Select the graph and/or table(s) to appear on a hardcopy plot.

commands.name: mmemory_catalog_query
commands.description: List the names of the files in memory.

commands.name: mmemory_store_state
commands.description: Save an instrument state to mass storage. The string is the filename.

commands.name: mmemory_load_state
commands.description: Recall an instrument state from mass storage. The string is the filename.

commands.name: mmemory_store_trace
commands.description: Store an ASCII list of trace and frequency values to a file. The character argument is the formatted data trace CH<1|2>FDATA and the string is the filename.

commands.name: mmemory_copy
commands.description: Copy a file. string1 is the source file, string2 is the destination file.

commands.name: mmemory_delete
commands.description: Delete a file. The string is the filename.

commands.name: mmemory_initialize
commands.description: Format a disk. The optional string is the mass storage device (MEM: internal memory, or INT: internal floppy disk), the char is the disk format (DOS), and the num is the interleave factor.

commands.name: mmemory_msis
commands.description: Select a mass storage device. Choose MEM: (internal memory) or INT: (internal floppy disk drive).

commands.name: status_operation_condition_query
commands.description: Read the Operational Status condition register.

commands.name: status_operation_event_query
commands.description: Read and clear the Operational Status event register.

commands.name: status_operation_enable
commands.description: Set and query bits in the Operational Status enable register.

commands.name: status_operation_averaging_event_query
commands.description: Read and clear the Averaging status event register.

commands.name: status_operation_measuring_event_query
commands.description: Read and clear the Measuring status event register.

commands.name: status_questionable_event_query
commands.description: Read and clear the Questionable Status event register.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Set and query bits in the Questionable Status enable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_limit_event_query
commands.description: Read and clear the Limit Fail event register.

commands.name: status_device_event_query
commands.description: Read and clear the Device Status event register.

commands.name: status_device_enable
commands.description: Set and query bits in the Device Status enable register.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Set bits in most enable and transition registers to their default state.

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Perform a system preset; the same as the front-panel PRESET key.

commands.name: system_error_query
commands.description: Query the error queue; returns the error number and message.

commands.name: system_version_query
commands.description: Query the SCPI version of the analyzer. Use *IDN? to query the firmware revision.

commands.name: system_date
commands.description: Set the year, month and day of the real-time clock.

commands.name: system_time
commands.description: Set the hour, minute and second of the real-time clock.

commands.name: system_beeper_immediate
commands.description: Instruct the analyzer to beep. Optional arguments are frequency (Hz), duration (seconds) and volume (0 to 1).

commands.name: system_communicate_gpib_address
commands.description: Set the analyzer's HP-IB address. A delay of 5 seconds is required before a command is sent to the new address.

commands.name: system_communicate_gpib_echo
commands.description: Turn on/off HP-IB mnemonic echo.

commands.name: system_communicate_gpib_hcopy_address
commands.description: Set the address of an HP-IB printer or plotter for hardcopy output.

commands.name: system_set_lrn_query
commands.description: Query or set the instrument state as a learn string (BLOCK).

commands.name: system_set_lrnlong_query
commands.description: Query or set the instrument state, data, and calibration as a learn string (BLOCK); similar to save/recall.

commands.name: system_key
commands.description: Send key names that execute the same functions as front-panel keys.
